name: Minimal Supersymmetric Standard Model
summary: A spacetime supersymmetry between bosons and fermions, broken at a scale near the electroweak scale, gives the Higgs mass technical naturalness through cancellation of quadratic divergences between SM particles and their superpartners. With R-parity, the lightest superpartner is a stable dark-matter candidate. Gauge couplings unify cleanly under MSSM running. LHC null results have pushed the natural SUSY scale above ~1-2 TeV, eroding but not eliminating the framework.

addresses_puzzles.note: Solves the technical-naturalness problem if M_SUSY ~ EW scale. Post-LHC bounds (M_SUSY > 1-2 TeV for colored superpartners) introduce a residual little hierarchy of ~few %; whether SUSY still 'solves' or merely 'softens' the puzzle depends on which definition of naturalness one accepts.

R-parity-conserving MSSM has the lightest neutralino as a WIMP DM candidate; direct-detection nulls have squeezed the parameter space but not closed it (well-tempered, focus point, coannihilation regions remain).
Three-loop precision running with MSSM particle content closes the gauge-coupling unification mismatch to <~1% near 2e16 GeV; this is the most data-supported feature of MSSM.

Exact SUSY would cancel vacuum-energy contributions; broken SUSY at M_SUSY ~ TeV leaves a residual ~M_SUSY^4, which is ~10^60 too large relative to the observed Lambda. Listed for graph completeness; not a viable solution.

touches_benchmarks.note: Decoupling limit reproduces SM Higgs couplings; post-LHC viable parameter space sits in this regime.
MSSM with light electroweakinos and sleptons can naturally accommodate the Fermilab + BNL g-2 result, depending on tan(beta) and the slepton spectrum.
Generic MSSM phases generate EDMs at 1-loop; nEDM and electron-EDM bounds force CP-violating phases to be small (the SUSY CP problem).

LZ 2024 (4.2 tonne-yr) constrains spin-independent neutralino-nucleon cross-sections to <9.2e-48 cm^2 at the most-sensitive m_chi ~ 36 GeV; viable MSSM neutralino DM remaining is mostly bino-like with coannihilation, well-tempered, or focus-point regions.
SUSY-GUT contributions to dimension-5 proton-decay operators (p -> K nu) saturate Super-Kamiokande bounds in much of the natural parameter space.

new_fields: full set of superpartners (squarks, sleptons, gauginos, higgsinos)
introduces.note: The full unconstrained MSSM has 105 parameters beyond the SM (Haber 1997). Constrained models (mSUGRA/CMSSM) reduce this to ~5, but at the cost of theoretical assumptions. We use the unconstrained count for the compression denominator; this is precisely why MSSM is becoming hard to motivate post-LHC.

history: Wess-Zumino (1974); Dimopoulos-Georgi (1981) MSSM; Dimopoulos-Raby-Wilczek (1981) gauge unification. Dominant BSM paradigm 1985-2015. Post-LHC Run 1+2 nulls (2012-2018) demoted from 'mainstream' to 'niche' for low-scale natural SUSY; high-scale (PeV) and split SUSY remain.

references.citation: Haber, 'The status of the minimal supersymmetric Standard Model and beyond' (1997)
